name: Indonesia
bestResult: Round of 16 (1938, as Dutch East Indies)

facts.label: AMATEURS ON A BOAT
facts.story: The 1938 squad wasn't Indonesia — it was the Dutch East Indies, a colonial side stocked with doctors, students, and clerks who'd never had a professional league or a real training camp. They sailed for weeks to reach the Netherlands, played a couple of warm-up friendlies against Dutch club sides, then boarded a train to Reims. Captain Achmad Nawir, a doctor, wore his reading glasses onto the pitch for the biggest match of his life.

facts.label: ONE MATCH, FOREVER
facts.story: Because 1938 used a straight knockout draw, the Dutch East Indies' 0–6 loss to Hungary on June 5 was their entire World Cup — one match, then gone. Nearly a century later that record stands unbroken: no nation has ever played fewer World Cup matches. Nawir later wrote in his journal that the sport was about joy, not the 'grim faces of purely success-hunting diehards.'

1938.headline: Asia's First World Cup Kit

annotations.label: HUNGARY'S HEAD START
annotations.story: Hungary needed just 13 minutes to seize control of the match at Reims, and the Dutch East Indies — playing on pure amateur grit against a team stacked with professionals — never recovered, going down 0–6 in the only World Cup match the nation has ever played.

1938.homeColors: orange shirt (the Dutch national color), white shorts, light blue socks — no official kit records survive, this is reconstructed from black-and-white match photos
